reasoning: The sink at class-bsr-admin.php line 288 is confirmed: `echo wp_strip_all_tags( $_POST['bsr-sysinfo'] )` outputs attacker-controlled POST data as the body of a text/plain download. The auth gate is real and correct: the handler is on admin_post_bsr_download_sysinfo (requires WP login), BSR_Utils::check_admin_referer() calls check_admin_referer() (nonce validated) AND bsr_enabled_for_user() which requires current_user_can('manage_options') — administrator level. CSRF is prevented. The textarea in bsr-help.php pre-fills with BSR_Compatibility::get_sysinfo() but the POST handler trusts $_POST['bsr-sysinfo'] directly, so an admin with a valid nonce can substitute arbitrary text content in the downloaded diagnostic file. wp_strip_all_tags is not a meaningful guard for text/plain content. Impact is confined: only an administrator can exploit this, and the consequence is a falsified diagnostic artifact (no code execution, no XSS, no privilege escalation, no impact on other users). The finding's characterization of reachability and severity is accurate.
